name: Indemnification
description: Promises to defend, hold harmless, and pay for losses caused by specified events or breaches.

name: Limitation of Liability
description: Caps and exclusions on damages — direct, indirect, consequential, lost profits — recoverable under the contract.

name: Warranties / Disclaimer
description: Express warranties about the product or service, and disclaimers of implied warranties.

name: Force Majeure
description: Excuses non-performance caused by events outside a party's reasonable control.

name: Termination for Cause
description: Right to end the contract due to material breach, insolvency, or other defined triggers.

name: Termination for Convenience
description: Right to end the contract without cause, typically on notice.

name: Term and Renewal
description: Length of the contract and conditions for automatic or elective renewal.

name: Confidentiality
description: Restrictions on the use and disclosure of confidential information shared under the agreement.

name: Intellectual Property Ownership
description: Allocation of ownership of pre-existing and newly created intellectual property.

name: License Grant
description: Scope, exclusivity, territory, and term of any license granted to use IP or content.

name: Non-Compete
description: Restriction on engaging in competing activities during or after the relationship.

name: Non-Solicitation
description: Restriction on soliciting employees, contractors, or customers of the other party.

name: Governing Law
description: Choice of the substantive law that governs the contract.

name: Dispute Resolution / Arbitration
description: Forum, procedure, and rules for resolving disputes — courts, arbitration, or mediation.

name: Jurisdiction and Venue
description: Where suit may be brought, and consent to personal jurisdiction.

name: Assignment
description: Whether and how a party may transfer its rights or obligations under the contract.

name: Notices
description: How formal notices under the contract must be delivered and when they take effect.

name: Entire Agreement
description: Statement that the written contract is the complete agreement and supersedes prior understandings.

name: Severability
description: What happens if a provision is found unenforceable — typically the rest survives.

name: Waiver
description: Limits on how a party's failure to enforce a right affects future enforcement.

name: Amendment
description: How the contract may be modified — usually written and signed by both parties.

name: Compliance with Laws
description: Obligation to comply with applicable laws, regulations, and policies.

name: Anti-Corruption / FCPA
description: Restrictions on bribery and corrupt practices, often referencing the FCPA and similar laws.

name: Data Protection / Privacy
description: Obligations to handle personal data in accordance with privacy laws (GDPR, CCPA, etc.).

name: Audit Rights
description: Right to inspect books, records, or facilities to verify compliance with the contract.

name: Payment Terms
description: Pricing, invoicing, due dates, late payment consequences, and taxes.

name: Insurance
description: Required types, amounts, and conditions of insurance coverage.
